packs.name: Classic Chaos
packs.instruction: Read it out loud. The active player answers or completes the challenge.

cards.text: What is the pettiest hill you would absolutely die on?
What is your most irrational everyday fear?
Give the group your best reality-show confessional about something that happened today.
What purchase made you feel rich for absolutely no reason?
Do an impression of someone in the group until they guess who it is.
What is the weirdest compliment you have ever received?
What is one completely useless skill you are strangely proud of?
Pitch your life as a trashy reality show in ten seconds.
What is the most embarrassing song you know every word to?
What is something you pretend to understand but absolutely do not?
Read your last harmless text like it is a dramatic movie monologue.
What is the dumbest way you have ever injured yourself?
What food opinion would get you kicked out of the group chat?
Create a new warning label for the player to your left.
What is the strangest thing currently living in your Notes app?
Which celebrity would play you in a movie made for almost no budget?
Sell the nearest random object like you are on a late-night infomercial.
What is your most chaotic travel story?
What tiny inconvenience turns you into a full villain?
Invent a fake scandal about yourself and hold a press conference.
What is the longest you have avoided a task that took under five minutes?
What is your most dramatic overreaction that you can admit now?
Give everyone in the group a ridiculous award title.
What trend did you swear you would never join and then absolutely joined?
What is something you believed for way too long?
Describe your dating personality using only three menu items.
What is your most suspiciously specific red flag?
What would your personal warning siren announce?
Let the group rename you for the next three cards.
What is the funniest thing you have done just to avoid an awkward conversation?

packs.name: Most Likely To
packs.description: Point, accuse and defend yourself immediately.
packs.instruction: Count to three. Everyone points to one player.

cards.text: Who is most likely to accidentally start a group argument and then fall asleep?
Who is most likely to survive a reality show the longest?
Who is most likely to create a business after one compliment?
Who is most likely to say “I’m fine” while clearly planning a dramatic exit?
Who is most likely to get banned from a neighborhood Facebook group?
Who is most likely to flirt their way out of trouble?
Who is most likely to bring twelve unnecessary items on a two-day trip?
Who is most likely to cry at a commercial and deny it?
Who is most likely to investigate a stranger’s entire life online?
Who is most likely to turn a simple dinner into a four-hour event?
Who is most likely to become famous for something completely accidental?
Who is most likely to have a secret second family in another reality?
Who is most likely to start dancing in a public place?
Who is most likely to make friends with the hotel staff?
Who is most likely to lose their phone while holding it?
Who is most likely to get into an argument with a self-checkout machine?
Who is most likely to become the villain of a family group chat?
Who is most likely to remember exactly what you said three years ago?
Who is most likely to order the same meal every single time?
Who is most likely to make a five-minute story last forty minutes?
Who is most likely to plan a vacation before checking their bank account?
Who is most likely to adopt another animal without asking?
Who is most likely to win an argument using absolutely incorrect information?
Who is most likely to send a risky text and immediately throw their phone?
Who is most likely to turn a hobby into a full company?
Who is most likely to have the most tabs open right now?
Who is most likely to say they hate drama while standing directly inside it?
Who is most likely to accidentally expose the group’s secret?
Who is most likely to make the best emergency contact?
Who is most likely to need a snack before making any major decision?

packs.name: Would You Rather
packs.description: Two terrible options. No hiding in the middle.
packs.instruction: Everyone chooses one option, then explain the suspicious reasoning.

cards.text: Would you rather have your camera roll shown to the group or your search history read aloud?
Would you rather be trapped on a cruise with an ex or seated beside them on a ten-hour flight?
Would you rather lose your phone for a week or give up coffee for a month?
Would you rather always arrive thirty minutes early or twenty minutes late?
Would you rather have one amazing outfit forever or a closet full of questionable choices?
Would you rather be famous for a scandal or unknown for your greatest achievement?
Would you rather know every secret about your friends or have them know every secret about you?
Would you rather only communicate through voice notes or only through reaction GIFs?
Would you rather redo your worst date or relive your most embarrassing night out?
Would you rather have unlimited travel with terrible hotels or luxury hotels but never leave your state?
Would you rather let the group choose your haircut or your next vacation?
Would you rather never use social media again or have every post approved by the group?
Would you rather be the funniest person in every room or the hottest?
Would you rather always know when someone is lying or never know when they are annoyed?
Would you rather have your life narrated by a drag queen or a sports commentator?
Would you rather share one bathroom with eight people or one bedroom with four?
Would you rather have a rewind button for conversations or a mute button for people?
Would you rather accidentally like a ten-year-old photo or send a screenshot to the person in it?
Would you rather be able to teleport but always arrive sweaty or fly but only five feet above the ground?
Would you rather give up your favorite food or your favorite streaming show?
Would you rather have a perfect memory or be able to forget anything on command?
Would you rather live without music or without television?
Would you rather switch phones with the player on your right or closets with the player on your left?
Would you rather have your most dramatic thought appear as subtitles or be spoken by Siri?
Would you rather host every holiday or never be invited to one again?
Would you rather date someone exactly like you or your complete opposite?
Would you rather be stuck in traffic with no air conditioning or at brunch with no coffee?
Would you rather have unlimited money for travel or unlimited money for home projects?
Would you rather repeat the same amazing year forever or keep moving into completely unknown years?
Would you rather win every argument or always know when to stop arguing?

packs.name: Red Flag or Just Gay?
packs.description: A highly scientific investigation into questionable behavior.
packs.instruction: Vote: red flag, harmless chaos, or somehow both.

cards.text: They have fourteen unread texts but post Stories all day.
Their mattress is directly on the floor, but the lighting is incredible.
They say all of their exes are crazy.
They still share a streaming password with an ex.
They call every friend “babe” and insist it means nothing.
They have no top sheet and seem proud of it.
They refuse to make plans more than two hours in advance.
Their phone is always face down.
They own twelve plants and every one of them is dying.
They describe themselves as “brutally honest.”
They say they hate drama but know everyone’s business.
They have matching tattoos with three different people.
They disappear for six hours and return with a new hobby.
They send seven-minute voice notes instead of answering one question.
They use astrology to explain why they forgot your birthday.
They have never replaced a roll of toilet paper.
They want a joint vacation before they know your last name.
They still have every dating app “just to look.”
They order for the table without asking.
They clap when the plane lands.
They have a close-friends list with more than two hundred people.
They say “I don’t really do labels” after six months.
They post inspirational quotes immediately after starting an argument.
They know their rising sign but not their credit score.
They own a ring light but no bedside lamp.
They bring a full photo shoot to a casual brunch.
They introduce you as “my friend” after telling everyone you are together.
They insist every vacation needs a matching outfit.
They have an unread email count over fifty thousand.
They say “trust me” before every terrible idea.

packs.name: Astrology Chaos
packs.description: Whose chart is it anyway? Point to the human embodiment.
packs.instruction: Everyone points to the player this sounds like.

cards.text: Needs to be right even when they’re asking a question.
Loves chaos until it’s time to clean it up — then suddenly becomes a Virgo.
Has deep emotional intelligence but forgets to text back.
Can plan an entire trip in five minutes, but takes three weeks to unpack.
Is either everyone’s therapist or everyone’s problem — no middle ground.
Gets moody when the vibe feels off but calls it “intuition.”
Says they hate attention, yet somehow ends up with the microphone.
Can’t focus until the environment looks like a Pinterest board.
Has the confidence of a fire sign but the anxiety of a water sign.
Will start a business because of one compliment.
Uses humor as both armor and weapon — often in the same sentence.
Feels deeply, but won’t admit it until three margaritas in.
Constantly “just checking in” but really wants to know the gossip.
Acts chill, secretly keeps receipts like a Scorpio with Wi-Fi.
Has an opinion about everything, including your rising sign.
Gives great advice they do not personally follow.
Starts every project with “This will be quick” — famous last words.
Emotionally reactive, recovers fast, then forgets the fight ever happened.
Attracts weird coincidences and blames it on the universe doing “bits.”
Wants everyone to get along but low-key loves a little drama.
Says they’re spontaneous but actually researched it for two weeks.
Falls in love through shared playlists and eye contact.
Can’t let someone else drive without judging their route.
Is both the storm and the calm after it.
Looks chill in photos, full existential meltdown behind the scenes.
Will reorganize the kitchen to feel emotionally stable again.
Thinks being vulnerable is hot until someone actually does it.
Laughs in serious conversations to diffuse tension — it never works.
Gives main-character speeches in their head daily.
Once cried over something beautiful and blamed “Mercury retrograde.”
